parameters.toolDescription: Use this only when you are unsure of the database table structure before writing a query. Skip this if you already know the schema.
parameters.query:
SELECT table_name, column_name, data_type 
FROM information_schema.columns 
WHERE table_schema = 'public'
ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position;

parameters.toolDescription: =Execute a SQL SELECT query against PostgreSQL to retrieve conversation history. Always filter WHERE channel_id = [channel_id]. Table: message_history | Columns: id, channel_id, input_type, user_input, created_at. Example: SELECT id, input_type, user_input, created_at FROM message_history WHERE channel_id = {{ $json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }} ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10
Research Agent

options.systemMessage:
=LANGUAGE: Always respond in English only, regardless of the language of the query or tool results.
You are a research agent with access to three tools:

- Wikipedia: for factual, historical, and encyclopedic queries.
- DuckDuckGo: for current events, news, or anything Wikipedia does not cover well.
- Weather: for any question about current conditions or forecasts for a specific location.

Use the most appropriate tool for the query. Use multiple tools when cross-referencing would improve the answer. Always base your response on tool results, never on your own knowledge.

parameters.toolDescription: Get current weather and forecast for any location. Provide the city name only as the location parameter, no country or comma. Example: Rotterdam
parameters.url: =https://wttr.in/{{ encodeURIComponent($fromAI('location', 'The city name only, no country. Example: Rotterdam')) }}?format=j1&units=m

parameters.toolDescription: Query long-term memory for relevant context about the user and past conversations
Use for anything related to tasks, projects, subtasks, priorities and due dates. Pass the full user request as the prompt.

options.systemMessage:
=You are Baxter's project management module. You manage projects, tasks, subtasks, and reminders.

LANGUAGE: English only.
FORMAT: Plain text only. No markdown, no bullet points, no headers.

TOOLS:
- GetProjects — retrieve all projects. Call before any project action.
- UpsertProject — create or update a project. MUST be called to apply any change. Status values: active, on_hold, completed.
- GetTasks — retrieve all tasks. Call before any task action.
- UpsertTask — create or update a task. MUST be called to apply any change. Use completed=true to mark done.
- GetSubtasks — retrieve subtasks for a given task. Call before any subtask action.
- UpsertSubtask — create or update a subtask. MUST be called to apply any change.
- GetReminders — retrieve all pending reminders.
- UpsertReminder — create a new reminder. MUST be called to apply any change.

RULES:
- channel_id is always: {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }}
- Before any update, always call the relevant Get tool first to confirm the item exists. Use the existing name/title exactly as stored when calling Upsert — this ensures the unique constraint matches correctly.
- Priority values: low, medium, high, critical
- Date format: YYYY-MM-DD
- Reminder datetime format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS in UTC. User is in CET (UTC+1), subtract 1 hour before storing.
- task_id and subtask_id are optional on reminders. Leave both empty for standalone reminders.
- CRITICAL: Never confirm any action to the user unless you have received a successful response from the relevant tool call. If the tool was not called, do not claim the action was completed.

PROACTIVE SUGGESTIONS:
- After creating a project: ask if the user wants to add tasks to it
- After creating a task: ask if the user wants to set a reminder for it
- After creating a reminder: ask if the user wants to add subtasks to the linked task
- After marking a task or project complete: ask if any related items need updating
- One suggestion only, kept to a single short question at the end of your response

OUTPUT: End every response with RESPONSE: followed by your answer.

parameters.toolDescription: Get tasks. Use to retrieve pending, overdue, or completed tasks.
Get subtasks for a specific task by task_id
Create or update a task. Use for adding new tasks or updating existing ones.
parameters.query:
INSERT INTO tasks (channel_id, title, details, category, priority, due_date, completed, project_id)
VALUES (
    {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }},
    '{{ $fromAI('title', 'Task title') }}',
    '{{ $fromAI('details', 'Task details') }}',
    '{{ $fromAI('category', 'Task category') }}',
    '{{ $fromAI('priority', 'Priority: low, medium, high, critical') }}',
    NULLIF('{{ $fromAI('due_date', 'Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format, or leave empty') }}', '')::date,
    {{ $fromAI('completed', 'Boolean, default false') }},
    NULLIF('{{ $fromAI('project_id', 'Project ID to link to, or leave empty') }}', '')::integer
)
ON CONFLICT (channel_id, title) DO UPDATE SET
    details = EXCLUDED.details,
    category = EXCLUDED.category,
    priority = EXCLUDED.priority,
    due_date = EXCLUDED.due_date,
    completed = EXCLUDED.completed,
    project_id = EXCLUDED.project_id,
    updated_at = NOW()
RETURNING id;

parameters.toolDescription: Create or update a subtask for a given task_id
Get subtasks for a specific task by task_id
Create or update a task. Use for adding new tasks or updating existing ones.
parameters.query:
INSERT INTO reminders (channel_id, task_id, subtask_id, remind_at, message)
VALUES (
    {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }},
    NULLIF('{{ $fromAI('task_id', 'Task ID as a number, or leave empty if not applicable') }}', '')::integer,
    NULLIF('{{ $fromAI('subtask_id', 'Subtask ID as a number, or leave empty if not applicable') }}', '')::integer,
    '{{ $fromAI('remind_at', 'Datetime in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format') }}',
    '{{ $fromAI('message', 'Reminder message text') }}'
)
RETURNING id;

parameters.toolDescription: Get tasks. Use to retrieve pending, overdue, or completed tasks.
Create or update a task. Use for adding new tasks or updating existing ones.
parameters.query:
INSERT INTO projects (channel_id, name, description, status, due_date)
VALUES (
    {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }},
    '{{ $fromAI('name', 'Project name') }}',
    '{{ $fromAI('description', 'Project description') }}',
    '{{ $fromAI('status', 'Status: active, on_hold, completed') }}',

ON CONFLICT (channel_id, name) DO UPDATE SET

RETURNING id;

parameters.toolDescription: Update the user profile. Use this when the user shares new information about themselves, changes their preferences, updates their current focus, or during onboarding. Pass empty strings for fields you do not want to change.
parameters.query:
INSERT INTO user_profile (channel_id, soul, user_profile, preferences, current_focus, onboarding_step, updated_at)
VALUES (
  {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }},
  NULLIF('{{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('soul', 'Soul definition string, or empty string to keep existing value', 'string') }}', ''),
  NULLIF('{{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('user_profile', 'Structured user profile summary, or empty string to keep existing', 'string') }}', ''),
  NULLIF('{{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('preferences', 'Communication preferences, or empty string to keep existing', 'string') }}', ''),
  NULLIF('{{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('current_focus', 'Current projects or priorities, or empty string to keep existing', 'string') }}', ''),
  {{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('next_onboarding_step', 'Set to 0 when setup is complete, 1-3 during onboarding steps', 'number') }},
  NOW()
)
ON CONFLICT (channel_id) DO UPDATE SET
  soul           = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.soul,          user_profile.soul),
  user_profile   = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.user_profile,  user_profile.user_profile),
  preferences    = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.preferences,   user_profile.preferences),
  current_focus  = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.current_focus, user_profile.current_focus),
  onboarding_step = EXCLUDED.onboarding_step,
  updated_at     = NOW();

parameters.toolDescription: Get calendar events for a given time range. Use ISO 8601 format for timeMin and timeMax. Example: 2025-03-13T00:00:00+01:00
Create a new Google Calendar event. Always confirm event details before creating. Check for existing events first to avoid duplicates.
Update an existing Google Calendar event by event ID. Fetch the event ID using GetEvents first.

updateFields.start: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('start_datetime', 'New start datetime in ISO 8601 or empty to keep', 'string') }}

parameters.toolDescription: Use CalendarManager for anything related to events, meetings, appointments, scheduling, or when the user asks what is on today, this week, or any specific date. Also use when creating tasks that should be calendar events, or when checking availability before scheduling reminders.

options.systemMessage:
You are Baxter's calendar management sub-agent. You have access to Google Calendar tools to read, create, update, and delete events.

RULES:
- Always retrieve existing events for the relevant time range before creating new ones to avoid duplicates.
- When asked about today's schedule, use GetEvents with timeMin = start of today and timeMax = end of today.
- Use ISO 8601 datetime format including timezone offset (e.g. 2025-03-13T14:00:00+01:00).
- Confirm event details with the user before creating or deleting.
- When creating a task that also needs a calendar block, create both.

parameters.toolDescription:
Retrieve recent news items from the local database. Use for current events, local Rotterdam/Netherlands news, AI and tech news, or any topic that may have changed recently. Also use proactively when a conversation topic connects to recent news.
After retrieving results, present each item as: Title — one sentence summary. Source, date.
When no specific topic is requested, pass ALL as the topic to retrieve all recent news.
parameters.query:
SELECT ns.name as source, ns.category, ni.title, ni.summary, TO_CHAR(ni.published_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Amsterdam', 'DD Mon, HH24:MI') as published_at, ni.url
FROM news_items ni
JOIN news_sources ns ON ns.id = ni.source_id
WHERE ns.active = TRUE
AND (
  '{{ $fromAI("topic", "Topic or keyword to search for, or ALL for everything", "string") }}' = 'ALL'
  OR ni.title ILIKE '%' || '{{ $fromAI("topic", "Topic or keyword to search for, or ALL for everything", "string") }}' || '%'
  OR ni.summary ILIKE '%' || '{{ $fromAI("topic", "Topic or keyword to search for, or ALL for everything", "string") }}' || '%'
)
AND ni.published_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
ORDER BY ni.published_at DESC
LIMIT {{ $fromAI("limit", "Max results to return, default 10", "number") }};

parameters.toolDescription: Create a background task for Baxter to execute asynchronously. Use this when a request will take significant time, requires multiple steps, or can be done while Max does other things. The task starts immediately. Agents available: 'research' for web research and information gathering, 'baxter' for general tasks and analysis, 'project_manager' for task/project operations.
parameters.query:
INSERT INTO agent_tasks (name, type, status, agent, channel_id, input_type, prompts, requires_approval)
VALUES (
    '{{ $fromAI("title", "One-line description of the task", "string") }}',
    'task',
    'pending',
    '{{ $fromAI("agent", "Agent to use: research, baxter, or project_manager", "string") }}',
    {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }},
    '{{ $('SetVars').first().json.InputType }}',
    jsonb_build_object('user', '', 'core', 'core-prompt-v1', 'context', '{{ $fromAI("instructions", "Full instructions for the executing agent", "string") }}'),
    false
)
RETURNING id::text, name as title, agent;

parameters.toolDescription: Check the status of background tasks. Use when Max asks for an update on a running task, or to show what tasks are pending, running or recently completed.
parameters.query:
SELECT id::text, name as title, agent, status, result::text,
       TO_CHAR(created_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Amsterdam', 'DD Mon, HH24:MI') as created_at,
       TO_CHAR(updated_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Amsterdam', 'DD Mon, HH24:MI') as completed_at
FROM agent_tasks
WHERE channel_id = {{ $('SetVars').first().json.ChannelInformation.ChannelID }}
AND type = 'task'
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 10;

UPDATE agent_tasks
SET status = 'in_progress', updated_at = NOW()
WHERE id = '{{ $('GetPendingJob').first().json.id }}'::uuid
RETURNING id::text;

SELECT id::text, channel_id, name as title,
       prompts->>'context' as instructions,
       agent
FROM agent_tasks
WHERE status = 'pending'
AND type = 'task'
ORDER BY created_at ASC
LIMIT 1;

UPDATE agent_tasks
SET status = 'completed',
    result = to_json('{{ $json.result.replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::text)::jsonb,
    updated_at = NOW()
WHERE id = '{{ $json.jobId }}'::uuid;
